title: Chisocheton perakensis
intro: Chisocheton perakensis, also known as the Perak chisocheton, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Chisocheton perakensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chisocheton perakensis is often used in herbal preparations such as tea, tinctures, and infusions to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Chisocheton perakensis is a species of plant that belongs to the family Rutaceae. This family is commonly known as the citrus or rue family, which consists of flowering plants like citrus fruits and rue. Chisocheton perakensis is further classified within the genus Chisocheton, which includes several other species of plants. The classification of Chisocheton perakensis is based on its characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships among different plant species within the family Rutaceae.
